title: Pediatricians, General
description: Diagnose, treat, and help prevent diseases and injuries in children. May refer patients to specialists for further diagnosis or treatment, as needed.

tasks.text: Prescribe or administer treatment, therapy, medication, vaccination, and other specialized medical care to treat or prevent illness, disease, or injury in infants and children.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine children regularly to assess their growth and development.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Treat children who have minor illnesses, acute and chronic health problems, and growth and development concerns.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine patients or order, perform, and interpret diagnostic tests to obtain information on medical condition and determine diagnosis.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advise patients, parents or guardians, and community members concerning diet, activity, hygiene, and disease prevention.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Explain procedures and discuss test results or prescribed treatments with patients and parents or guardians.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collect, record, and maintain patient information, such as medical history, reports, or examination results.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor patients' conditions and progress and reevaluate treatments as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Direct and coordinate activities of nurses, students, assistants, specialists, therapists, and other medical staff.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan and execute medical care programs to aid in the mental and physical growth and development of children and adolescents.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Refer patient to medical specialist or other practitioner when necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Teach residents or medical students about pediatric topics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide consulting services to other physicians.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate on patients to remove, repair, or improve functioning of diseased or injured body parts and systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan, implement, or administer health programs or standards in hospitals, businesses, or communities for prevention or treatment of injury or illness.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct research to study anatomy and develop or test medications, treatments, or procedures to prevent or control disease or injury.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare government or organizational reports of birth, death, and disease statistics, workforce evaluations, or medical status of individuals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
